qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Users Admin can modify and de-admin arbitrary administrator accounts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a distinct broken-authorization flaw in `objects/userAddNew.json.php` and `Permissions::canAdminUsers()`. It does not involve the advisory's SSRF root cause, attacker-controlled `downloadURL`, or server-side URL fetching in `objects/aVideoEncoder.json.php`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's authenticated SSRF root cause in `objects/aVideoEncoder.json.php`, where an authenticated caller supplies `downloadURL` and the server fetches it without proper validation.
evidence: The advisory JSON and referenced fix commit `384ef2548093f4cbb1bfac00f1f429fe57fab853` describe authenticated SSRF via `downloadURL` in `objects/aVideoEncoder.json.php`.

The upstream AVideo source under `artifacts/runtime/avideo-upstream/objects/aVideoEncoder.json.php` contains the relevant `downloadVideoFromDownloadURL()` server-side fetch path, confirming the advisory component and root cause location.

The only final qualifying finding in the run manifest, `users_admin_can_modify_admin_accounts`, targets `objects/userAddNew.json.php`, `objects/user.php`, and `Permissions::canAdminUsers()` to overwrite administrator accounts; it does not describe or exercise any server-side fetch of attacker-chosen URLs.
